clauses.obligation: Non-exclusive license to use Marks and System for each authorized restaurant
clauses.duration: Unlimited license; 10-year Operating Term per restaurant

clauses.obligation: Agreement expires if no restaurant authorized within 2 years of Effective Date
Must lease approved site within 210 days of Site Search Implementation Date
Submit site approval request at least 90 days before lease commitment date
Franchisor responds to site proposal within 30 days; silence = withheld
Must use and maintain equipment, computers, HW/SW per Manual; must participate in AI policies
Schedule training 90+ days before opening; complete 3+ weeks before opening; pass certification tests
Failure to complete training or pass testing = termination without refund
Must not disclose Confidential Information or Trade Secrets; employees must sign NDA
Must not disclose Agreement negotiation terms except as required by law
No Asian-style food (Chinese, Japanese, Thai, Korean) at same Facility except Panda Express items
clauses.trigger: During term and any successor term
exceptions: Independent catering if items not similar to Panda

clauses.obligation: 8% royalty on Gross Volume or $4,000 minimum per Royalty Period
Royalties in addition to Proprietary Product markup (10% surcharge)
$10M CGL; $3M auto; $10M cyber; property all-risk; workers comp; occurrence basis required
If terminated for your default: 24 months Royalties based on prior 12 Royalty Periods + 90 days Minimum Royalty per terminated restaurant
If you terminate Operating Term per Section 16.4B (venue closure): 12 months Royalties based on prior 12 Royalty Periods
clauses.trigger: Voluntary Operating Term termination due to venue closure

clauses.obligation: If liquidated damages held unenforceable, franchisor entitled to consequential damages
3 defaults within 12 months = automatic termination regardless of individual cures
Sell equipment/Trade Dress/signage/fixtures to franchisor at net book value (5-year amortization) if requested
De-identify restaurant; return all IP/Manuals; cancel fictitious name registrations within 10 days
Franchisor has power of attorney re: phone numbers, domain names, directory listings if not transferred within 10 days
Right of first refusal — 30 days; can substitute cash for other payment forms
Single 5-year successor term; may require materially different agreement; $10,000 fee; general release
Personal guaranty may be required of all direct/indirect owners; spouse/domestic partner co-sign possible
Franchisee indemnifies franchisor for all claims; franchisor indemnifies for IP infringement claims arising from proper use
Must begin reconstruction within 60 days after force majeure; failure = non-curable default
Mediation then binding AAA arbitration in LA County; jury waiver; class action waiver; loser pays
California law; Federal Arbitration Act preempts; noncompete governed by state where breach occurs
Written amendment only; Manual changes unilateral and binding without acceptance
Year 5: Brand Refresh to current design/trade dress/color/brand image. Year 10: Complete update possible as condition for successor term or transfer
Must participate in AI policies and specifications franchisor develops for restaurant operations
8-month prior notice required; if less notice, pay early termination fee based on average daily Gross Revenue
If default uncured, franchisor can manage restaurant(s) for 7% of Gross Volume + costs; 10 days notice to discontinue

distinctive_clauses: AI policy participation requirement — unusual for QSR franchises
Liquidated damages of 24 months royalties on franchisor-initiated termination for cause
12 months royalties even on franchisee-initiated venue closure termination
Mandatory minimum royalty ($4,000/period) regardless of sales
No exclusive territory in any form
59 JV restaurants where PRG affiliate is minority owner — blurs franchisee/company boundary
Airplane assets ($61.2M) held by franchise entity but used by parent companies
